findings.issue: The batched gate names the decision file and schema (draft_gate_decision_<batch>.json) and links review logs, but does not specify gate_id, owns (which step regenerates a skill on 'revise'), or invalidates (what becomes stale when a batch is approved with per-skill revisions) — all required fields per gate_design_patterns.md's 'Gate specification (required per gate)' table.
findings.fix: Add the three missing fields to the Author gate section: a stable gate_id (e.g. draft_batch_gate_<n>), an explicit owns statement (a 'revise' disposition routes that skill back to step 1 of the per-skill cycle), and an invalidates statement (approving a batch with edits marks that skill's downstream review and any dependent BUILD_PLAN items stale).

findings.location: Writing doctrine (from Anthropic's skill-creator, verbatim in spirit)
findings.issue: The doctrine bullets cite skill-creator concepts (progressive disclosure, generalization) without restating the grounding library's scope-limit language (skill-creator's methodology is scoped to 'skill authoring for Claude-family agents' per edu_grounding_library.md), leaving the anchor's scope implicit rather than stated per L1's 'state the application, not just the citation' rule.
findings.fix: Add a one-line scope note under the doctrine header pointing to edu_grounding_library.md's scope-limit text for skill-creator.
findings.resolution: skill-creator scope limit lives in the grounding library entry it cites

findings.issue: The gate's rendering modality (chat-native structured collection vs. a generated HITL web page) is unstated, so it cannot be confirmed whether WCAG 2.2 keyboard/screen-reader operability (critical flag 9) applies or is satisfied; gate_design_patterns.md cites POSED's guided web app as the reference gate implementation, so if page-draft's batch gate is ever rendered the same way, accessibility is currently unaddressed in this section.
findings.fix: State the gate's rendering modality explicitly, and if a web page is used, add a one-line accessibility requirement consistent with the pattern other PAGE stages use.
findings.resolution: Rendering modality is harness-dependent; WCAG bar applies whenever pages are generated (umbrella rule 8)

resolution_pass.legend: status=fixed: addressed in the named release; status=accepted: deliberate, documented deviation or deferred minor
